rationale.root_cause: They identified the FLAW at the memcpy in the bad function (around line 42) that copies sizeof(structCharVoid) bytes into structCharVoid.charFirst (16 bytes), overwriting voidSecond/voidThird. They explained the resulting pointer corruption and crash when printLine dereferences the corrupted voidSecond.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed changing the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst), exactly matching the required local fix. They even showed a concrete diff applying this change.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended using bounded-copy APIs that tie the copy size to the destination capacity (e.g., snprintf/strlcpy or a helper/macro deriving the array size) and replacing raw char[N] plus manual memcpy with a bounded abstraction. This represents the destination as a bounded buffer so the copy length is tied to the field capacity.
